original_hypothesis.title: Operator Overwrite of Claimable Agent Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The onGenesisSuccess function in Genesis.sol can be called multiple times by the OPERATION_ROLE. Subsequent calls overwrite the claimableAgentTokens mapping instead of adding to it. A malicious or compromised operator can redirect users' unclaimed tokens to their own addresses.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: State transitions that distribute assets should be terminal or strictly additive. Allowing an operator to overwrite user claims violates the trust model that users will receive what they earned during genesis.
preconditions: Users have not yet claimed their tokens
OPERATION_ROLE is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Missing terminal state flag for success path
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Between initial success and user claims

operational_questions: Is the OPERATION_ROLE a multisig or an automated backend wallet?
Is there monitoring for multiple onGenesisSuccess calls?
validation_requirements: Test calling onGenesisSuccess twice with different distribution arrays

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker waits for a successful genesis event
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Before users claim, attacker calls onGenesisSuccess again
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Attacker sets their own addresses in the distribution array, overwriting legitimate claims

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Theft of user allocations, severe reputational damage
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - stolen tokens must be recovered or re-minted

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Multiple onGenesisSuccess calls for the same genesisId

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
